claimText: Semaglutide, marketed as Wegovy, functions as an appetite suppressant by imitating the intestinal hormone glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1).
evidenceExcerpt: Semaglutide acts as an appetite suppressant by imitating the intestinal hormone glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1).
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism of action of emerging weight-loss drugs.
distractorHooks: Acts as a metabolic fat-burner rather than an appetite suppressant
Imitates insulin or ghrelin instead of GLP-1

claimText: Glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) is an intestinal hormone that is naturally released after eating to make a person feel fuller.
evidenceExcerpt: GLP-1 is naturally released after eating and makes people feel fuller.
examUse: Statement-based question on human digestive hormones and satiety.
distractorHooks: Released before eating to stimulate hunger
Produced in the brain instead of the intestine

claimText: Tirzepatide, used in weight loss drugs like Mounjaro, differs from semaglutide by mimicking the functions of both GLP-1 and GIP hormones.
evidenceExcerpt: Other weight loss drugs like Mounjaro use tirzepatide, which mimics both GLP-1 and GIP functions.
examUse: Correct-pair or comparison question distinguishing between different active pharmaceutical ingredients in weight loss drugs.
distractorHooks: Functions by blocking the absorption of dietary fats in the intestines
